prompt:
You are an engineering experiment design assistant. Return JSON only.

Task:
{
  "task_id": "task_demo_002",
  "paper_id": "paper_demo_002",
  "domain": "additive_manufacturing",
  "task_type": "experiment_design",
  "goal": "Design one feasible experiment to study how laser power affects porosity in a DED NiTi process.",
  "available_resources": {
    "equipment": [
      "DED machine",
      "SEM",
      "XRD",
      "precision saw",
      "mounting press",
      "grinding and polishing kit"
    ],
    "materials": [
      "NiTi powder",
      "argon gas",
      "mounting resin"
    ],
    "constraints": [
      "Use only listed equipment and materials.",
      "Use explicit numeric laser power levels.",
      "Keep all non-power settings fixed."
    ]
  },
  "evidence_chunk_ids": [
    "paper_002_chunk_01"
  ],
  "evidence_summary": "The process uses a DED machine with argon shielding gas. Samples can be sectioned, mounted, ground, and polished for SEM cross-section imaging. SEM and XRD are used for characterization. A previously validated stable operating window is available for selecting feasible laser power levels.",
  "evidence": [
    {
      "source_id": "paper_002_chunk_01",
      "text": "The process uses a DED machine with argon shielding gas. Samples can be sectioned, mounted, ground, and polished for SEM cross-section imaging. SEM and XRD are used for characterization. A previously validated stable operating window is available for selecting feasible laser power levels."
    }
  ]
}
